jokes.text: Any recommendations for the best place to buy a girl's bike on the upper West Side? She's eight. - I'm sorry... What's a girl's bike? Is that like a girl doctor? Go back to Saudi Arabia, Hitler!
jokes.setup: Innocent request for bike shop recommendations
jokes.punchline: Immediate escalation to sexism and Nazi comparisons
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of online comment toxicity with perfect escalation from innocent question to absurd hostility

jokes.text: You're buying a bike but not a helmet? The head is where the child's brain is. Why don't you get educated, double Hitler!
jokes.setup: Continuation of toxic online behavior
jokes.punchline: 'Double Hitler' as an escalation, plus the absurdly obvious 'head is where the child's brain is'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with 'double Hitler' and hilariously obvious medical fact

jokes.text: My two-year-old is super gay, and we love him more than a straight child because he doesn't rape!
jokes.setup: Online parenting discussion
jokes.punchline: Absurd logic connecting toddler sexuality to criminal behavior
jokes.explanation: Dark absurdist humor typical of 30 Rock's extreme takes on social issues

jokes.text: Yesterday I moved Kabletown's customer service to a part of India that has no phones. We're now providing the same level of service to our subscribers at zero the cost.
jokes.setup: Corporate efficiency announcement
jokes.punchline: Admitting they're providing no service by moving to a place with no phones
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack Donaghy corporate sociopathy - logical yet completely immoral efficiency

jokes.text: And then we'll be able to get even better trophy wives... Half-Asian ones!
jokes.setup: Discussion of corporate success benefits
jokes.punchline: Casual racism/sexism as a perk of wealth
jokes.explanation: Typical 30 Rock corporate villain behavior but not particularly clever

jokes.text: I never told you this, but I once came up late at night looking for Jack, and you were in a wedding dress dancing with one of his suits.
jokes.setup: Casual revelation to explain Jonathan's behavior
jokes.punchline: Jonathan's creepy obsession manifested as wedding dress suit-dancing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonathan characterization - specific, weird, and completely believable for him

jokes.text: Stay-at-home Lemon. To what do I owe the pleasure? Did you already run out of things to do today? - Pfft! What? No. You are.
jokes.setup: Jack's condescending question about Liz's new status
jokes.punchline: Liz's terrible 'I know you are but what am I' comeback
jokes.explanation: Perfectly shows Liz being flustered and reverting to childish responses under pressure

jokes.text: To keep away frankensteins, which, as far as we know, worked.
jokes.setup: Explaining Tracy's fire-setting behavior
jokes.punchline: Treating the absence of Frankensteins as evidence the fires worked
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy Jordan logic delivered deadpan - impossible to argue with the 'evidence'

jokes.text: I ran this morning for 30 minutes. - Does that include dry heaving? - And wet.
jokes.setup: Liz claiming to have exercised
jokes.punchline: Jack's implication she's out of shape, confirmed by Liz admitting to both types of heaving
jokes.explanation: Good character-driven humor about Liz's fitness level with efficient escalation

jokes.text: He has a degree in ethno-musicology from wesleyan, so he's a receptionist in a dental office.
jokes.setup: Explaining Criss's employment
jokes.punchline: Expensive liberal arts education leading to menial job
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about modern economics and liberal arts education value

jokes.text: Hogcock! Which is a combination of 'hogwash' and 'poppycock.'
jokes.setup: Jack needing to express disbelief
jokes.punchline: Creating a portmanteau and immediately explaining it
jokes.explanation: Classic Jack Donaghy - creating unnecessarily complex solutions to simple problems

jokes.text: If my mother told me that everything I had been raised to believe was a lie and then died, I'd be like, 'say what?'
jokes.setup: Describing a traumatic life event
jokes.punchline: Massively understated reaction to profound trauma
jokes.explanation: Liz's typical emotional understatement in the face of serious issues

jokes.text: A city built on the religion of capitalism, and I am its high priest, looking down on the swinish multitude.
jokes.setup: Jack describing his view from his office
jokes.punchline: Grandiose self-comparison to a religious figure of capitalism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack megalomania - sees himself as literally godlike in his corporate role

jokes.text: Even they must acknowledge me... As a god. - And this makes you happy? - It should.
jokes.setup: Jack's grandiose claims about his power
jokes.punchline: His admission that godlike power doesn't actually make him happy
jokes.explanation: Reveals Jack's existential crisis beneath his success - achieving everything but happiness

jokes.text: You know, I'm reminded of something Yoda once said. Mmm! Ohh! Dark times are these.
jokes.setup: Liz setting up what sounds like wisdom
jokes.punchline: Bad Yoda impression with backwards syntax
jokes.explanation: Standard pop culture reference humor - not particularly clever but fits Liz's character

jokes.text: We thought maybe contestants could win money instead of penis punches.
jokes.setup: Adapting a Japanese game show for American audiences
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of physical abuse as the original prize
jokes.explanation: Good cultural humor about extreme Japanese game shows vs. American sensibilities

jokes.text: He promised me he'd always be there for me, no matter what! - Sometimes things change. - And yet you still say stupid stuff to me all the time and suck at carrying boxes!
jokes.setup: Tracy complaining about Kenneth's changed status
jokes.punchline: Tracy pointing out Grizz and Dotcom haven't improved despite their unchanged status
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Tracy's childish logic and treatment of his entourage

jokes.text: You've always had the body for it.
jokes.setup: Liz mentioning concentrating on being a mom
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's inappropriate comment about her body being suited for motherhood
jokes.explanation: Kenneth's oblivious inappropriate comment fitting his naive character

jokes.text: Woman, writer, New York... Those are all on my list of TV no-no words.
jokes.setup: Liz describing her show concept
jokes.punchline: Kenneth rejecting the exact premise of 30 Rock itself
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about the show's own premise being unmarketable - very self-aware

jokes.text: They don't want to watch some angry New York crankypants make that face... exactly.
jokes.setup: Kenneth describing what audiences don't want
jokes.punchline: Liz making exactly the face he's describing
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual comedy - Liz proving Kenneth's point by her reaction

jokes.text: Shows where a guy gets a drink thrown in his face, and then he turns to his dog and says, 'don't even say it.' To his dog!
jokes.setup: Kenneth describing the kind of shows he wants to make
jokes.punchline: Absurd premise of talking to a dog about relationship advice
jokes.explanation: Good satire of lowest-common-denominator TV with Kenneth's innocent enthusiasm

jokes.text: I will tell the other gays your real ages!
jokes.setup: Jenna threatening the makeup crew
jokes.punchline: Revealing ages as the ultimate threat in gay culture
jokes.explanation: Jenna's typical narcissistic threats with cultural awareness of gay community dynamics

jokes.text: I will stop being an actress when the earth stops spinning on Kabbalah monster's fingernail.
jokes.setup: Jenna declaring her commitment to acting
jokes.punchline: Completely nonsensical metaphor involving Kabbalah
jokes.explanation: Peak Jenna absurdity - completely makes no sense but delivered with total conviction

jokes.text: Good-bye forever, you factory reject dildos.
jokes.setup: Jenna making her dramatic exit
jokes.punchline: Bizarre insult comparing crew to defective sex toys
jokes.explanation: Another wonderfully specific and inappropriate Jenna insult - creative vulgarity

jokes.text: Inga, you have to warn me when we have an important guest. I'm afraid I'm gonna have to spank you again.
jokes.setup: Jack addressing his assistant about protocol
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of repeated workplace spanking
jokes.explanation: Jack's casual inappropriate behavior - fits character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Where are all the baby pigeons?
jokes.setup: Kenneth sharing what he's been thinking about
jokes.punchline: Completely random urban mystery observation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth randomness - actually thought-provoking observation delivered innocently

jokes.text: It's like when a pig says, 'if I can communicate with you telepathically, do I have a soul?' And then you're like, 'no. Duh. Into the slaughtering chute.'
jokes.setup: Kenneth making an analogy about self-awareness
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal of pig's existential crisis followed by immediate slaughter
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark Kenneth moment - philosophical depth followed by casual brutality, perfectly delivered

jokes.text: As I was taught at Six Sigma... Analyze, strategize, succeed. 'A.S.S.' I'm going to crush this problem... With my ass.
jokes.setup: Jack applying business methodology to personal problems
jokes.punchline: The acronym spells ASS, leading to literal interpretation
jokes.explanation: Classic Jack corporate speak with juvenile wordplay - perfectly in character

jokes.text: That's Tracy... 't' as in the drink, 'r' as in the pirate noise, 'a' as in the Fonzie noise, 'c' as in sea monster, 'y' as in why do we even make friends if they're gonna let you down when we need them the most?
jokes.setup: Tracy spelling his name phonetically
jokes.punchline: Escalating from simple phonetics to existential crisis about friendship
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Tracy moment - starts mundane, escalates to emotional breakdown, perfect character work

jokes.text: 'J' as in the birds I'm afraid of...
jokes.setup: Tracy continuing to spell 'Jordan'
jokes.punchline: Revealing his fear of birds/jays
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Tracy's many irrational fears, fits the character perfectly

jokes.text: I did, but I got fired... For stupidity.
jokes.setup: Online debate about working vs. stay-at-home parenting
jokes.punchline: Blunt admission of being fired for incompetence
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective self-deprecating humor in the online comment format

jokes.text: For example, the rapper T.I., who wrote, 'better get on yo job, tell 'em. Haters get on yo job, nougats.' at least, I think he was saying 'nougats.'
jokes.setup: Citing rap lyrics as work inspiration
jokes.punchline: Mishearing explicit lyrics as candy reference
jokes.explanation: Good cultural humor about mishearing explicit lyrics - relatable and clever

jokes.text: I'll be the one wearing a purple sweater and wrapping a baby swing around some skank's neck.
jokes.setup: Online argument escalating to real-world meetup
jokes.punchline: Specific violent threat using playground equipment
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation of mommy blog warfare to physical violence with playground irony

jokes.text: And if you can, get hit by a car. You get to spend a couple days in the hospital, and they'll give you soup.
jokes.setup: Weather report advice for dealing with cold
jokes.punchline: Recommending hospitalization for free soup
jokes.explanation: Darkly absurd practical advice that reveals economic desperation masked as helpfulness

jokes.text: Nancy, I know this is unconventional, but I really think a group relationship could work. - What you're proposing is a sin, Jack, but she is wicked hot.
jokes.setup: Jack proposing a threesome
jokes.punchline: Nancy's moral objection immediately undermined by physical attraction
jokes.explanation: Standard temptation vs. morality joke with Catholic guilt twist

jokes.text: My foot hurts, but I'm okay. I think this injury has given me crime-solving powers. It's law & order: Mind beauty.
jokes.setup: Jenna getting hurt during filming
jokes.punchline: Believing minor injury grants detective abilities and creating nonsense spinoff title
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna narcissism - turning minor accident into delusions of grandeur and TV opportunity

jokes.text: I am going to the city of angels, a veterinary hospital where I get dog sedatives that help me relax when I fly.
jokes.setup: Jenna dramatically announcing she's going to LA
jokes.punchline: Calling it 'city of angels' but meaning a vet hospital for dog drugs
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - sets up dramatic LA departure but reveals drug addiction pit stop first

jokes.text: Good-bye forever, you soup line at a gay homeless shelter.
jokes.setup: Jenna's final dramatic exit
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely specific and offensive farewell insult
jokes.explanation: Another wonderfully weird Jenna insult - specific, inappropriate, and completely unpredictable

jokes.text: Since when do you listen to T.I.? - That message board is for moms. I thought you were a lady.
jokes.setup: Liz questioning Criss about rap knowledge
jokes.punchline: Criss explaining he was on mommy blogs and made assumptions about other posters
jokes.explanation: Good revelation that explains earlier forum fight - Criss's innocent mistake

jokes.text: Sitting at a desk makes me crazy, so I keep getting up and getting coffee. Now I'm all jittery and weird and... bird!
jokes.setup: Criss explaining why he hates office work
jokes.punchline: Coffee addiction making him paranoid and easily startled
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy and relatable office worker anxiety with caffeine jitters

jokes.text: Her kids were so bored, I just wanted to jump over that divider and play waiting games with them, like carpet adventure. - Or that one where your hands are spiders. - Hand spiders.
jokes.setup: Criss describing his jealousy of stay-at-home parenting
jokes.punchline: Specific games they both know, revealing their shared understanding
jokes.explanation: Sweet moment showing their compatibility and shared childlike sensibilities

jokes.text: If you were a dude, you would not even be thinking that. It's okay to want to work. One of us has to. We just got it backwards. You're... the dad.
jokes.setup: Liz feeling guilty about wanting to work
jokes.punchline: Criss pointing out gender role reversal in their relationship
jokes.explanation: Smart social commentary about gender expectations and relationship dynamics

jokes.text: I do like ignoring your questions while I try to watch TV. - Exactly!
jokes.setup: Discussion of their role reversal
jokes.punchline: Liz admitting to stereotypical 'dad' behavior
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor about traditional gender roles and relationship dynamics

jokes.text: A show about a dentist's office where the sassy hygienist says things like, 'I'm turning 30... Again.'
jokes.setup: Criss suggesting a show concept for Liz
jokes.punchline: Terrible sitcom premise with hacky one-liner
jokes.explanation: Good satire of lowest-common-denominator TV with obvious workplace comedy premise

jokes.text: When I went up to Ken's office earlier, I saw his schedule on his assistant's pornography box! - You mean computer?
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining how he knows Kenneth's schedule
jokes.punchline: Tracy calling computer a 'pornography box'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy technological ignorance - reducing computers to their basest use

jokes.text: Unless you need us to hate the new Taylor swift album. That girl has feelings. - You're wrong, Dotcom!
jokes.setup: Dotcom and Grizz offering to help Tracy with anything
jokes.punchline: Their one limitation being appreciating Taylor Swift's emotional depth
jokes.explanation: Funny character moment showing Dotcom and Grizz's unexpected cultural opinions

jokes.text: Neither is talking two catholic beauties into a delicious vanilla-caramel sex swirl, but I did it.
jokes.setup: Jack arguing that upward spirals are possible
jokes.punchline: Describing his threesome in ice cream flavor terms
jokes.explanation: Classic Jack sexual bragging with creative food metaphor - perfectly in character

jokes.text: Oh, thank you, you two, for blowing my brains.
jokes.setup: Nancy expressing gratitude after the threesome
jokes.punchline: Innocent phrasing with sexual double meaning
jokes.explanation: Basic sexual innuendo - functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: You string cheese with a tooth stuck in it.
jokes.setup: Jack insulting Kenneth
jokes.punchline: Bizarre food-based insult
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific and weird Jack insult - creative imagery

jokes.text: They burned you in effigy! The hair went up like a Roman candle.
jokes.setup: Reporting on Occupy Wall Street's response to Jack
jokes.punchline: Specific detail about his hair being particularly flammable
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy about Jack's distinctive hair being a fire hazard

jokes.text: By having to come down here and listen to us.
jokes.setup: Politician describing how they'd punish Jack Donaghy
jokes.punchline: Admitting that listening to politicians is actually punishment
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware political humor about politicians' own boring nature

jokes.text: God, I feel like I have a 'macropenis' right now.
jokes.setup: Jack feeling powerful from the political attention
jokes.punchline: Creating medical term for feeling virile
jokes.explanation: Typical Jack pseudo-medical sexual terminology - fits character

jokes.text: America wants to see... John hardly. He loves his family, but he hates the rat race. He's 'hardly working.'
jokes.setup: Liz pitching a TV show concept
jokes.punchline: Terrible pun-based character name and premise
jokes.explanation: Intentionally bad TV pitch showing Liz's desperation - weak pun is the point

jokes.text: And I don't mean those two santas I saw kissing on the subway.
jokes.setup: Kenneth mentioning contract clauses
jokes.punchline: Random inappropriate Santa observation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth non-sequitur - completely random yet somehow believable

jokes.text: Shut your chin slit.
jokes.setup: Kenneth making a compliment
jokes.punchline: Liz's creative insult for mouth
jokes.explanation: Great creative insult - specific, anatomically weird, and very quotable

jokes.text: You eastern European knockoff Mr. Potato heads.
jokes.setup: Jenna insulting airport workers
jokes.punchline: Specific toy-based cultural insult
jokes.explanation: Another creative Jenna insult combining cultural stereotypes with children's toys

jokes.text: I know what it is to blow up overnight. And I'm not talking about my gout.
jokes.setup: Tracy describing sudden fame
jokes.punchline: Clarifying he doesn't mean his medical condition
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy health problem reference with medical misdirection

jokes.text: So, like the snakes I kept in my dressing room, I release you.
jokes.setup: Tracy releasing Kenneth from his promise
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to releasing actual snakes he kept
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy weirdness - sweet moment undermined by casual mention of keeping snakes

jokes.text: Am I supposed to drop this on the floor?
jokes.setup: Kenneth emotional about Tracy's gesture
jokes.punchline: Practical confusion about tissue disposal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth moment - emotional beat interrupted by practical etiquette concern

jokes.text: I'm looking for six figures, eight if you're counting cents, which I fell for once before... Not cool, the gap.
jokes.setup: Liz stating her salary requirements
jokes.punchline: Admitting to falling for deceptive pricing at The Gap
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating humor about being fooled by retail pricing tricks

jokes.text: I pissed off my enemies... Pelosi, Maddow, Baldwin.
jokes.setup: Jack listing his accomplishments
jokes.punchline: Casually naming liberal media figures as personal enemies
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack political positioning - casual name-dropping of liberal 'enemies'

jokes.text: You're just an alcoholic with a great voice.
jokes.setup: Liz realizing Jack doesn't know what he's talking about
jokes.punchline: Reducing his entire persona to drinking and vocal quality
jokes.explanation: Sharp character insight disguised as insult - actually quite accurate about Jack

jokes.text: When I met you, I was perfectly happy with what I had... Eating night cheese and transitioning my pajamas into daywear.
jokes.setup: Liz blaming Jack for changing her life
jokes.punchline: Describing her previous lifestyle as gloriously slovenly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz self-awareness - 'night cheese' is iconic, 'transitioning pajamas' is brilliantly specific

jokes.text: I used to be a shark, and then you 'unsharkulated' me.
jokes.setup: Jack blaming Liz for his changes
jokes.punchline: Creating the word 'unsharkulated' to describe losing his edge
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jack neologism - perfectly captures the concept with corporate-speak efficiency

jokes.text: I called you up here for one meeting seven years ago, and you kept coming up.
jokes.setup: Jack and Liz arguing about their relationship
jokes.punchline: Jack describing the entire show's premise as Liz being clingy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on the show's entire seven-year run as one extended meeting

jokes.text: So we ruined each other? Good to know.
jokes.setup: Realizing their mutual negative influence
jokes.punchline: Casual acceptance of their destructive relationship
jokes.explanation: Good Liz deflection - using understatement to avoid emotional processing

jokes.text: There! You're no longer special to him. Get out! Get out of our lives! Yes!
jokes.setup: Jack and Liz declaring their relationship over
jokes.punchline: Jonathan's celebration of Liz's dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonathan moment - his jealousy finally vindicated

jokes.text: The same company that suggested we get flush buddies to save water.
jokes.setup: Pete commenting on NBC's business decisions
jokes.punchline: Reference to bizarre water-saving bathroom policy
jokes.explanation: Good corporate absurdity - weird enough to be believable from NBC

jokes.text: This is Dan silversmith with Carolina mutual. I can't talk right now. I'm with a customer.
jokes.setup: Phone call interruption
jokes.punchline: Pete's already moved on to new job during the conversation
jokes.explanation: Good Pete moment showing his practical approach to career transition

jokes.text: Like that machine Kathy Geiss invented that hugs old people. - My god, this will change elder care forever. - Hmm. Whoops. Nope.
jokes.setup: Liz making analogy about holding Tracy down
jokes.punchline: Brief hope for elderly care innovation immediately crushed
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and deflation - moment of hope for humanity immediately dashed

jokes.text: The only thing that can stop this show is an act of god or if some genius figured out a way to stall Just long enough...
jokes.setup: Tracy seemingly accepting his contractual obligation
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's planning sabotage while stating conditions
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy misdirection - appearing compliant while plotting sabotage

jokes.text: The night is young. And neither are you.
jokes.setup: Tracy warning Liz their battle isn't over
jokes.punchline: Casual age insult delivered as threat
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy insult delivery - threatening but ultimately about appearance

jokes.text: Eating night cheese and transitioning my pajamas into daywear.
jokes.setup: Liz describing her life before Jack
jokes.punchline: Was happy eating night cheese and wearing pajamas as clothes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz self-description - 'transitioning pajamas into daywear' is beautifully specific

jokes.text: Alphabetically after Lemon comes... Oh, no.
jokes.setup: Determining lunch picker by alphabet
jokes.punchline: Cerie's realization that Lutz comes next
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Lutz's revenge plot with dawning horror from Cerie

jokes.text: It's Manhattan's largest out-of-business women's blazer dump.
jokes.setup: Jack returning Liz's loyalty card
jokes.punchline: Describing a hilariously specific failed business
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz shopping habit - incredibly specific, pathetic, and completely believable

jokes.text: I assume that's code for a billionaire's soul-searching trip to tan penis island.
jokes.setup: Jack saying he's going away
jokes.punchline: Liz's cynical interpretation as wealthy person cliché
jokes.explanation: Great Liz cynicism with hilariously specific wealthy person destination

jokes.text: Most of tan penis island was destroyed in Sting's house fire.
jokes.setup: Liz mentioning the island
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to celebrity real estate disaster
jokes.explanation: Good callback with absurd celebrity scandal detail

jokes.text: According to the national weather service, you should, and I quote, 'leave work, get in your purple Bentley, and be home with your sharks before the tristate area gets slammed by what is being called snowicane'
jokes.setup: Weather emergency announcement
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific advice clearly targeting Tracy personally
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - professional weather report that's obviously been bought and paid for

jokes.text: What did you do to Al Roker to make him do that? - Let's just say his wife is on the board of a children's hospital, and they need a celebrity to host their annual gala, and I threatened to do it.
jokes.setup: Liz asking how Tracy corrupted the weather report
jokes.punchline: Threatening to ruin charity event as blackmail
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Tracy logic - threatening to help charity as a threat. Perfectly twisted.

jokes.text: I can't talk right now. I'm with a customer.
jokes.setup: Pete getting call from insurance company
jokes.punchline: Pretends Liz is customer to avoid dealing with real life
jokes.explanation: Good Pete character moment - his avoidance of real-world responsibilities

jokes.text: Did you pin her up against the wall? Were your shirts wet with rain?
jokes.setup: Jack asking about his argument with Liz
jokes.punchline: Jenna's romantic novel interpretation of conflict
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna moment - interpreting normal conflict through romance novel lens

jokes.text: When Conan dumped her, she dropped him forever. - Hey, Liz. - Come on, you can't pretend I don't exist. We dated for a year! We were gonna lose our virginity to each other! Now I'll never lose it!
jokes.setup: Jenna explaining Liz's grudge-holding
jokes.punchline: Conan's pathetic plea revealing their awkward past
jokes.explanation: Great celebrity cameo with embarrassing backstory that works on multiple levels

jokes.text: I'm afraid to even tell her that I frenched her dad on new year's.
jokes.setup: Jenna explaining her fear of Liz's grudges
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of kissing Liz's father
jokes.explanation: Peak Jenna inappropriate behavior casually mentioned as minor concern

jokes.text: I spent Christmas alone in the Hamptons, drinking scotch and throwing firecrackers at Billy Joel's dog!
jokes.setup: Jack describing his loneliness
jokes.punchline: Specific celebrity pet harassment as coping mechanism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack wealthy person depression - specific, petty, and completely believable

jokes.text: Daddy, please stop crying.
jokes.setup: Jack's emotional breakdown
jokes.punchline: Child's embarrassed plea during public meltdown
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Jack's public breakdown with innocent child reaction

jokes.text: I wrote it on flypaper. I'm five steps ahead of you, you sons of bitches!
jokes.setup: Lutz's name being stuck to someone
jokes.punchline: Revealing his elaborate sabotage plan
jokes.explanation: Great Lutz revenge moment - surprisingly clever sabotage from typically incompetent character

jokes.text: I've never met Mickey Rourke.
jokes.setup: Jenna making a comparison
jokes.punchline: Breaking character to admit she's been lying about celebrity connections
jokes.explanation: Moment of Jenna honesty about her fabricated celebrity stories

jokes.text: Good luck with, uh, Jake.
jokes.setup: Kenneth trying to be supportive
jokes.punchline: Getting Jack's name wrong after seven years
jokes.explanation: Typical Kenneth name confusion - stays true to his oblivious character

jokes.text: Go home to your wife and eight beautiful children. You're all so beautiful.
jokes.setup: Tracy making emotional goodbyes
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific family details for random crew member
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy emotional moment with unexpectedly specific personal knowledge

jokes.text: You changed your name to 'aardvark'? That's insane! - Shh. Shh, shh, shh, shh, shh. Blimpie's.
jokes.setup: Lutz beating the alphabetical system
jokes.punchline: Extreme name change for lunch revenge, delivered triumphantly
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Lutz moment - extreme dedication to petty revenge with perfect shushing delivery

jokes.text: ♪ I will never forget you ♪ ♪ rural juror ♪ What? What is that face?
jokes.setup: Jenna performing her song
jokes.punchline: Unsingable lyrics creating confusion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback to show's running gag about unpronounceable words in song form

jokes.text: To cry, you just clutch a shard of broken glass.
jokes.setup: Jenna explaining acting techniques
jokes.punchline: Self-harm as casual acting method
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna narcissism - casual admission of dangerous acting methods

jokes.text: If anything ever happens to me and you can't find my body but you can find this, don't overthink it. Just Bury the necklace and move on.
jokes.setup: Frank explaining his necklace
jokes.punchline: Casual instructions for his likely violent death
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank darkness - matter-of-fact instructions for his probable murder

jokes.text: Sweet, funny Elizabeth, your light always shown the brightest, baby. - You're coming to the show tonight, right? - I'll be watching. Not sure from where, but I'll be with you... In spirit.
jokes.setup: Jack's touching tribute to Liz
jokes.punchline: Ambiguous phrasing suggesting he might be dead
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection suggesting Jack's planning something dark while seeming sentimental

jokes.text: Because for seven years, you have yelled at me and turned the lights out on me when I was in the bathroom and written on me while I was sleeping, 'cause I was Lutz... Dumb, old, uncool, part-inuit, bisexual, 51-year-old Lutz!
jokes.setup: Lutz explaining his revenge motivation
jokes.punchline: Escalating list of specific abuse and random personal details
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lutz breakdown - escalating from workplace abuse to random identity details

jokes.text: Give it up for Liz Lemon, everybody! The least molested person in here.
jokes.setup: Liz being introduced at strip club
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment about her lack of sexual harassment
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Liz's relative safety in inappropriate venue

jokes.text: Anybody who's ever left me in my life just left... My dad going to get a pack of smokes and never coming back, all those foster parents talking about adopting me and never did.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining his abandonment issues
jokes.punchline: Specific childhood trauma as motivation for avoiding goodbyes
jokes.explanation: Genuine Tracy emotional moment grounding his behavior in real trauma

jokes.text: We were forced to be friends because of work. And we're probably not gonna hang out after this, all right? You'll say that you're gonna invite me to your house, and it's never gonna happen.
jokes.setup: Liz giving Tracy an honest goodbye
jokes.punchline: Brutally realistic assessment of workplace friendships
jokes.explanation: Perfect honest goodbye - acknowledging the artificiality of workplace relationships

jokes.text: Because the human heart is not properly connected to the human brain, I love you, and I'm gonna miss you.
jokes.setup: Liz continuing her honest goodbye to Tracy
jokes.punchline: Scientific explanation for emotional attachment despite practical considerations
jokes.explanation: Beautiful Liz moment - perfectly balances cynicism with genuine emotion through medical metaphor

jokes.text: At the end of act two, my character, constance justice, sings the title song. It's a tearful good-bye to her true love, Norman Blurder, the rural juror.
jokes.setup: Jenna describing her Broadway show
jokes.punchline: Impossible to pronounce character names that sound like 'Rural Juror'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback to Rural Juror - 'Norman Blurder' is perfect tongue-twister companion

jokes.text: Brian Williams needs a mirror on the floor of his bathroom. I guess you want that if you have a glass toilet.
jokes.setup: Kenneth explaining his post-show activities
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of celebrity's bizarre bathroom fixtures
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth casual weirdness - matter-of-fact about celebrity's strange lifestyle

jokes.text: Oh, god, that idiot. What did he do? - You are watching my video suicide note.
jokes.setup: Liz worried about Jack's state of mind
jokes.punchline: Jack's dramatic video reveal confirming her fears
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Jack's emotional crisis to apparent suicide threat

jokes.text: This is like what happened with my gynecologist all over again!
jokes.setup: Liz feeling responsible for Jack's apparent suicide
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to previous medical professional suicide
jokes.explanation: Dark Liz callback showing her pattern of feeling responsible for others' extreme actions

jokes.text: I want to be buried in it. So, if anything ever happens to me and you can't find my body but you can find this, don't overthink it. Just Bury the necklace and move on.
jokes.setup: Kenneth explaining his necklace
jokes.punchline: Casual instructions for his potential murder/disappearance
jokes.explanation: Dark Kenneth humor - matter-of-fact instructions for his potential murder are perfectly unsettling

jokes.text: I sure hope so, tray. I sure hope so.
jokes.setup: Tracy hoping Pete will forget him
jokes.punchline: Pete's sincere agreement to forget Tracy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pete response - honest desire to forget his traumatic work experience
